summary: Round-1 items (1) and (2) look fixed, but shutdown still does not reliably prevent orphaned listener descendants.

findings.title: Listener shutdown only signals the shell parent, so subprocess trees can survive daemon exit
findings.recommendation: Spawn listeners in their own process group and terminate the group on shutdown/restart (for example with `detached: true` and `process.kill(-child.pid, ...)` on POSIX, or an equivalent cross-platform tree-kill strategy). As written, `spawn('/bin/sh', ['-c', job.command])` means the tracked child may be just the shell; the later `SIGTERM`/`SIGKILL` at shutdown only targets that parent process, not any grandchildren started by the command.
